claim_short: 0305082 AOST Questions 3.15 3.17 qst3.3 qst3.5 hereditary structure of spreading models exactly n countably many ell_1 ell_2 uncountably many. Beanland Freeman Motakis 1408.0265 Theorem 1 explicitly solves the AOST questions: finite F gives exactly n ell_p spreading models, increasing sequence plus limit gives countably infinitely many, F={1,2} gives hereditary ell_1 and ell_2 without uncountably many spreading...
packet_title: Literature Already Answered: Hereditary Counts of Spreading Models
source_authors: G. Androulakis, E. Odell, Th. Schlumprecht, N. Tomczak-Jaegermann
source_question: Source paper: G. Androulakis, E. Odell, Th. Schlumprecht, and N. Tomczak-Jaegermann, "On the structure of the spreading models of a Banach space", arXiv:math/0305082. Locations: TeX labels qst3.3 and qst3.5; printed as Questions 3.15 and 3.17 on pages 19-20 of the source PDF. Covered questions: If every infinite-dimensional subspace admits ell_1 and ell_2 spreading models, must there be more, perhaps uncountably...
source_title: On the structure of the spreading models of a Banach space
